name: rockstarr-content
description: Rockstarr AI content drafting plugin. Five publishing lanes (researched blog, thought leadership, email newsletter, LinkedIn newsletter, LinkedIn polls) gated by the client's stack cadence, plus interview-driven case studies and repurposed derivatives. v0.6 adds the LinkedIn polls lane: draft-polls writes batched sets of 10 LinkedIn polls per persona with strict character-limit enforcement (question ≤140, options ≤30), an audience-altitude pre-check before drafting (the lane's #1 failure mode), style continuity from the most recent approved set, and a single-file-per-set output approved as one batch. Polls cadence is an enum (monthly | quarterly | on-demand | off) since per-month numerics don't fit the batched-set unit. Polls config (brand hashtag, persona list, voice rhythm, hashtag mix) lives in the LinkedIn polls subsection of style-guide.md. v0.5 added the strategic content-planning layer: seo-strategy outputs a canonical backlog of 25-32 prioritized blog topics organized into 4-5 pillar+supporting clusters; ideate-topics + content-calendar + outline-blog are backlog-aware. Researched-blog lane (v0.4) runs WebSearch research + 13-item SEO/GEO checklist as Pass 1 before stop-slop. Thought-leadership lane (v0.3) runs the canonical TL rubric as Pass 1 before stop-slop. Every lane drafts in the client's approved voice from first-party KB; third-party sources are cited inline and listed in References, never paraphrased as the client's voice. Requires rockstarr-infra v0.8.2+ for the shared blog-seo-geo reference; v0.8.1+ for the TL rubric; v0.4+ for the rest.
